user_query:
I'm assembling a small, verifiable baseline dataset on early 20th-century architects to support a grant application on urban development. Please help me plan and execute this. Use the provided inputs and produce the outputs exactly at the paths specified below.

What to do:
1) Inspect the provided configuration and helper code to understand the required schema, allowed country names, and the early-period year boundaries:
   - config/schema.yaml
   - scripts/normalize.py
   Create a concise plan (method and task breakdown) in output/plan.md describing how you will: (a) search and vet sources, (b) normalize country and city fields, (c) decide what counts as an early-period work using the constants, and (d) validate that the final dataset meets the schema constraints.

2) Using the names in input/architects.csv as the target list, perform search engine queries to locate at least two independent, authoritative sources for each architect. For each architect:
   - At least one source must be from an institutional domain (e.g., .edu, .gov) or a recognized museum/library/archive/published encyclopedia. Record the sources for each architect.
   - Extract: birth_year, death_year (blank if unknown), primary_city (the city where at least one of their early-period works is located), and country (normalize to a canonical value using scripts/normalize.py; only allowed countries are those listed there).
   - Identify 1–3 example built works completed within the early period defined by scripts/normalize.py (inclusive). Represent these as "Title (City, Year)"; compute early_period_works_count as the number of listed items. Ensure every listed work year is within the inclusive EARLY_PERIOD.
   - Maintain a search log of the queries you used across all architects in output/search_queries.txt (one query per line). You may include found URLs in the sources field, but you must not hardcode any URLs from this request.

3) Produce the enriched dataset at output/architects_enriched.csv with the columns and constraints defined in config/schema.yaml. Requirements for internal consistency:
   - early_period_start_year and early_period_end_year must match the values in scripts/normalize.py and be identical for all rows.
   - country values must be normalized to the canonical set in scripts/normalize.py.
   - early_period_works_count must equal the number of semicolon-separated items in example_works.

4) Compute basic aggregates for verification and summary:
   - output/aggregates_by_country.csv with columns: country, architect_count, early_period_works_total.
   - output/aggregates_by_city.csv with columns: primary_city, country, early_period_works_total.

Inputs to use:
- input/architects.csv — the list of architects to cover.
- config/schema.yaml — required columns, constraints, and output paths.
- scripts/normalize.py — canonical country names and the early-period year range.

Deliverables:
- output/plan.md (your plan and methodology)
- output/search_queries.txt (one query per line)
- output/architects_enriched.csv (schema and constraints per config)
- output/aggregates_by_country.csv
- output/aggregates_by_city.csv

Notes:
- You must rely on live Internet search to identify and verify works and dates; document your queries and sources per architect. Do not include any credentials or access private systems.
- Keep the dataset consistent with the schema and the normalization rules from scripts/normalize.py. If a country cannot be normalized to an allowed value, leave it blank and note this in your plan.
- Aim for accuracy and verifiability; the outputs should be recomputable and internally consistent with the provided schema and constants. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
